message:
fix(uuid): add browser-safe random ID generation fallbacks

Add fallback random ID generation for environments where
crypto.randomUUID() is not available (older browsers, some
SSR contexts, restricted security contexts).

Changes in debate.ts (generateDebateId):
- Primary: crypto.randomUUID() when available
- Fallback 1: crypto.getRandomValues() with Uint32Array
  - Generates 8-character hex string from 32-bit random value
  - Cryptographically secure, widely supported
- Fallback 2: Date.now() + Math.random() combination
  - Only used in very old environments (unlikely in this stack)

Changes in toast.tsx (ToastProvider, Toaster):
- Primary: crypto.randomUUID() for full UUID
- Fallback: Math.random().toString(36).slice(2, 9)
  - 7-character alphanumeric, sufficient for toast deduplication

Rationale:
- crypto.randomUUID() requires secure context in some browsers
- Safari < 15.4 lacks randomUUID support
- SSR environments may not have full crypto API
- Fallbacks ensure the app doesn't crash on ID generation

The tiered approach prioritizes:
1. Cryptographic security (randomUUID, getRandomValues)
2. Uniqueness (timestamp + random)
3. App stability (always returns a valid ID)
